label: Data Structure and Algorithm Subject
description: The subject where stack and queue are covered in unit three.

label: Unit 3
description: Contains topics such as stack and queue.

label: Stack
description: An abstract data type, commonly used in most programming languages. A linear data structure that follows a particular order (LIFO/FILO).

label: Queue
description: A data structure topic covered in unit three.

label: Abstract Data Type (ADT)
description: Means stack uses different types of operations, whose theory allows direct use of push and pop operations during implementation.

label: Homogeneous Items
description: Similar types of items that form an ordered group of elements.

label: Linear Data Structure
description: All the data or elements are arranged in a sequential manner or in linear fashion.

label: LIFO (Last In First Out)
description: The particular order in which operations are performed on a stack, where the last item added is the first one out.

label: FILO (First In Last Out)
description: An order that is the same as LIFO.

label: Top of the Stack
description: The location where elements are added to and removed from; the most recently added items are placed here.

label: Stack Implementation Methods
description: Ways to implement a stack, including by means of Array, Structure, Pointer, and Linked List.

label: Stack Size
description: Can either be fixed size or may have a sense of dynamic resizing.

label: Real Life Applications of Stack
description: Examples include stack of cafeteria trays, coins, shoe boxes, neatly folded shirts, books, stones, plates of tray, shipment in cargo, drawers, and railway yard.

label: Stack Representation
description: A conceptual diagram showing how data is inserted and deleted using operations.

label: Push Operation
description: Inserting elements one by one into the stack.

label: Pop Operation
description: Removing elements from the stack one by one.

label: Types of Stack
description: Mainly two types: implicit stack and explicit stack.

label: Implicit Stack
description: Built-in to every computer system, not implemented by a program or developer, used by the compiler to implement recursion, and stores information like return address, local variables, and parameters.

label: Explicit Stack
description: Implemented by programmers or developers, used by algorithms needing a stack, and examples include push/pop operations, infix to prefix expression conversion, recursive algorithms, and traversive algorithms.

label: Operations Performed on Stack (ADT)
description: A set of functions that can be performed on a stack including push, pop, peek, isFull, and isEmpty.

label: Peek Function
description: Gets the top data element from the stack without removing it.

label: isFull Function
description: Checks whether the stack is full.

label: isEmpty Function
description: Checks if the stack is empty.

label: Applications of Stack in Data Structure
description: Includes converting infix expression to postfix and prefix, evaluating postfix expressions, reversing strings, checking well-formed parentheses, processing subprogram/function calls, parsing, decimal to binary conversion, and backtracking algorithms.

label: Important Questions (SPPU Exam)
description: Frequently asked questions in the SPPU exam pattern, covering stack definition, implementation, implicit/explicit stack, recursion importance, and applications of stack.

label: Recursion
description: A functionality implemented by the compiler using the implicit stack; also relevant to explicit stack algorithms and an important question topic.

label: Stacks
description: A new chapter introduced in the presentation.

label: Presentation
description: Introduces the concepts of stacks.

label: Outline of Presentation
description: The outline of this presentation.

label: Definition of Stack
description: What a stack is all about.

label: Real Life Examples of Stacks
description: Some real life examples of stacks.

label: Stack as an ADT
description: What is a stack as an ADT (Abstract Data Type).

label: Stack Operations
description: Primary and secondary operations supported in case of stack.

label: Primary Operations
description: A category of stack operations.

label: Secondary Operations
description: Another category of stack operations.

label: Insertions
description: Allowed only at the end called the top of the stack in a stack.

label: Deletions
description: Allowed only at the end called the top of the stack in a stack.

label: Array
description: A type of linear data structure, like stack or linked list.

label: Linked List
description: A type of linear data structure, like stack or array.

label: Stack of Books
description: A real life example of stacks.

label: Stack of Coins
description: A real life example of stacks.

label: Access Object
description: To retrieve an object, usually the top most one in a stack example.

label: Add Object
description: To place an object, usually at the top most place in a stack example.

label: Glass Jar Analogy
description: A way to imagine a stack where objects are placed inside, making it easier to visualize restrictions.

label: Closed at Bottom
description: A characteristic of the glass jar in the analogy, implying no access from below.

label: Opened at Top
description: A characteristic of the glass jar in the analogy, implying access from above.

label: One Way to Insert/Delete
description: The singular method for modifying objects in a stack (from the top).

label: User Point of View
description: The perspective from which stack operations are considered when defining a stack as an ADT.

label: Implementation Details
description: How stack operations are performed, which is not of interest when defining a stack as an ADT from a user's perspective.

label: Top Operation
description: A secondary stack operation which simply returns the last inserted element without removing it.

label: Size Operation
description: A secondary stack operation which simply returns the size or the number of elements in the stack.

label: List of Commands
description: A sequence of stack commands to be executed.

label: Push(1)
description: A command to push the element '1' inside the stack.

label: Push(2)
description: A command to push the element '2' inside the stack.

label: Push(3)
description: A command to push the element '3' onto the stack.

label: Pop()
description: A command to delete the top most element from the stack.

label: IsFull()
description: A command to check if the stack is full.
